name: Garri / Eba (cassava swallow)
portionGuidance: One small fist-size mould, not a mountain.
pairingAdvice: Vegetable soup (efo, edikang ikong) and a protein.
frequency: A few times a week, small.
logicNote: Turns to sugar fast. Portion and soup pairing are everything.

name: Pounded Yam
pairingAdvice: Egusi or vegetable soup plus meat or fish.
logicNote: Smooth and dense; easy to overeat. Slow it with fibre and protein.

name: Amala (yam flour)
frequency: A few times a week, small.
logicNote: Slightly friendlier than white swallows but still a starch; portion matters.

name: Amala (plantain flour)
frequency: Better choice among swallows; still small.
logicNote: Often a smarter swap than cassava or yam swallow.

name: Fufu / Akpu (fermented cassava)
logicNote: High starch; pair and shrink the portion.

name: Semovita / Semolina swallow
logicNote: Refined wheat; spikes quickly.

name: Wheat swallow
frequency: A better swallow choice; keep portion sensible.
logicNote: More fibre than semo or eba; a common recommended swap.

name: Oat swallow
logicNote: One of the friendliest swallows; more fibre, slower rise.

name: Tuwo Shinkafa (rice swallow)
pairingAdvice: Miyan kuka or vegetable soup plus protein.
logicNote: Soft white rice paste; fast sugar.

name: Tuwo Masara (corn swallow)
logicNote: Corn-based; a touch friendlier than rice tuwo.

name: Starch (cassava, Niger Delta)
logicNote: Very dense starch; keep it small.

name: White Rice (boiled)
portionGuidance: Half your usual plate, not full.
frequency: Small, a few times a week.
logicNote: The classic spike. Cut portion, add fibre and protein to slow it.

name: Jollof Rice
pairingAdvice: Protein and a side salad or vegetables.
logicNote: Oil and tomato do not lower the sugar load; portion still rules.

name: Fried Rice
pairingAdvice: Protein; the mixed vegetables help a little.
logicNote: Vegetables in it help slightly, but it is still white rice.

name: Ofada Rice (local unpolished)
frequency: A friendlier rice; still mind portion.
logicNote: Less polished than white rice, so a slower rise. A good swap.

name: Brown Rice
frequency: A better everyday rice; keep portion sensible.
logicNote: More fibre than white rice; the smarter default swap.

name: Basmati Rice
logicNote: Lower GI than ordinary white rice.

name: Rice and Beans
portionGuidance: Moderate portion, more beans than rice.
frequency: Decent option; beans slow the rice.
logicNote: The beans add fibre and protein, which softens the rice spike.

name: Boiled Yam
pairingAdvice: Egg sauce, vegetable sauce, or fish stew.
logicNote: Dense starch; pair with protein and keep pieces few.

name: Fried Yam (dundu)
pairingAdvice: If eaten, a protein and pepper sauce.
logicNote: Starch plus deep frying. Worst of both.

name: Yam Porridge (asaro)
pairingAdvice: Lots of vegetables in it, plus fish.
logicNote: Load it with vegetables and protein to slow the starch.

name: Roasted Yam (boli yam)
pairingAdvice: Palm oil sauce with pepper and fish.
logicNote: Better than fried, still a dense starch.

name: Sweet Potato (boiled)
pairingAdvice: Egg sauce or vegetables and protein.
frequency: A friendlier tuber; keep it boiled, not fried.
logicNote: More fibre than white yam; a reasonable swap when boiled.

name: Irish Potato (boiled)
logicNote: Higher GI than sweet potato; mind portion.

name: Cocoyam (boiled)
logicNote: Lower GI than yam; a decent swap.

name: Boiled Plantain (unripe / green)
logicNote: Unripe means less sugar and a slower rise. Friendly.

name: Boiled Plantain (ripe / yellow)
logicNote: The riper and sweeter, the faster the sugar.

name: Fried Plantain (dodo)
logicNote: Ripe sugar plus frying oil. Spikes hard.

name: Unripe Plantain Porridge
pairingAdvice: Plenty vegetables and fish in it.
logicNote: Unripe plantain plus vegetables is a friendly Nigerian meal.

name: Plantain Chips (fried)
logicNote: Fried, salty, easy to overeat.

name: Popcorn (plain)
logicNote: Plain is OK in small amounts; caramel and sugar versions are red.

name: Abacha (African salad)
pairingAdvice: Fish, ugba, garden egg, vegetables in it.
logicNote: Cassava base; the vegetables and fish help.

name: Suya
logicNote: Spiced grilled meat; low carb. A great street-food choice.

name: Kilishi
frequency: OK; some versions add sugar or groundnut paste.
logicNote: Mostly protein; check for sweet coatings.

name: Nkwobi
logicNote: Cow foot in spicy sauce; low carb.

name: Boli (roasted plantain)
pairingAdvice: Groundnut or pepper sauce and fish.
logicNote: Unripe boli with groundnut is the friendlier version.

name: Chin Chin
logicNote: Fried sweet flour; spikes fast.

name: Puff Puff
logicNote: Fried sweet dough; one of the worst snacks.

name: Meat Pie
logicNote: Refined flour pastry; fast rise.

name: Sausage Roll / Gala
logicNote: Refined pastry around processed meat.

name: Buns
logicNote: Fried sweet dough.

name: Doughnut
logicNote: Fried, sugared dough.

name: Biscuits (sweet)
logicNote: Refined flour and sugar.

name: Cake
logicNote: Sugar and refined flour.

name: Ice Cream
logicNote: Concentrated sugar and dairy.

name: Cooked Beans (brown / honey)
pairingAdvice: Fish, egg, or a little plantain.
logicNote: High fibre and protein, slow rise. One of the best diabetic-friendly staples.

name: Ewa Agoyin
portionGuidance: Normal bowl, easy on the oil sauce.
frequency: Good, but watch the oily sauce.
logicNote: Beans are great; the heavy palm-oil sauce is the only caution.

name: Moi Moi (bean pudding)
pairingAdvice: Stands alone or with a salad.
logicNote: Steamed beans, protein-rich, slow rise. A top pick.

name: Akara (bean cakes, fried)
pairingAdvice: Pap is the problem, not the akara; pair with vegetables instead.
frequency: OK in moderation; mind the frying oil.
logicNote: Beans are friendly; deep frying is the only downside.

name: Beans Porridge
pairingAdvice: Fish or a little unripe plantain.
logicNote: Filling, high fibre, slow rise.

name: Okpa (Bambara nut pudding)
logicNote: Bambara nut, protein-rich, slow rise.

name: White Bread / Agege Bread
portionGuidance: Avoid; one thin slice at most.
pairingAdvice: If eaten, egg and vegetables, never alone.
logicNote: Soft refined flour; one of the fastest spikes.

name: Whole Wheat Bread
logicNote: Check the label; many wheat breads are mostly white flour.

name: Pap / Akamu / Ogi
pairingAdvice: Moi moi or akara to slow it down.
frequency: Occasional; never with sugar or condensed milk.
logicNote: Smooth corn starch, fast rise. The sugar people add is the danger.

name: Custard
logicNote: Like pap; danger is the added sugar and milk.

name: Oats (plain)
pairingAdvice: A few nuts; skip sugar and sweet milk.
logicNote: Plain oats are friendly. Flavoured or instant ones often have added sugar.

name: Golden Morn
logicNote: Often eaten very sweet; that is the problem.

name: Cornflakes
logicNote: Refined corn plus usually sugar and sweet milk.

name: Boiled Corn
logicNote: Pairing with pear or coconut slows the rise. Better than roasted.

name: Roasted Corn
logicNote: Roasting raises the GI slightly versus boiled.

name: Egusi Soup
pairingAdvice: Eat with a small swallow and plenty vegetables.
logicNote: Melon seed and vegetables; friendly. The swallow beside it is what to watch.

name: Ogbono Soup
logicNote: Low carb on its own; the swallow is the variable.

name: Efo Riro
logicNote: Vegetable-heavy and friendly.

name: Edikang Ikong
logicNote: Rich in vegetables and protein. One of the best.

name: Afang Soup
logicNote: Leafy and protein-rich; friendly.

name: Oha Soup
logicNote: Vegetable soup; watch only the thickener and swallow.

name: Bitterleaf Soup (Ofe Onugbu)
logicNote: Friendly vegetable soup.

name: Okra Soup (Okro)
frequency: Excellent; okra itself helps blood sugar.
logicNote: Okra is one of the friendliest vegetables for diabetics.

name: Vegetable Soup (mixed)
logicNote: More vegetables, better.

name: Banga Soup (palm fruit)
logicNote: Low carb; just rich in oil, so moderate the quantity.

name: Ewedu
pairingAdvice: Gbegiri and a small swallow with protein.
logicNote: Pure leafy soup; very friendly.

name: Gbegiri (beans soup)
logicNote: Bean-based, high fibre, slow rise.

name: Groundnut Soup (Miyan Gyada)
logicNote: Groundnut adds protein and slows the meal.

name: White Soup (Ofe Nsala)
logicNote: Light, low carb; watch only the yam thickener if used.

name: Pepper Soup
logicNote: Broth and protein, almost no carbs. A great choice.

name: Miyan Kuka (baobab leaf)
logicNote: Leaf-based, friendly.

name: Chicken (grilled / boiled)
pairingAdvice: Anything; remove skin for less fat.
logicNote: Grilled or boiled beats fried.

name: Beef (lean)
logicNote: Trim visible fat.

name: Goat Meat
logicNote: Lean red meat; friendly.

name: Fish (Titus / Mackerel / Tilapia / Catfish)
frequency: Excellent; one of the best proteins.
logicNote: Grilled, boiled, or peppered. Oily fish is especially good.

name: Eggs (boiled)
logicNote: No carbs, filling. Boiled beats fried.

name: Turkey
logicNote: Lean if skin removed.

name: Snail
logicNote: Lean and friendly.

name: Prawns / Crayfish
logicNote: Low carb seafood.

name: Pomo (cow skin)
frequency: OK; low nutrition but harmless for sugar.
logicNote: No carbs; fine for blood sugar.

name: Liver
logicNote: Protein-rich; fine for blood sugar.

name: Fried Chicken / Fried Fish
logicNote: The protein is fine; deep frying is the only caution.

name: Ugu (pumpkin leaf)
logicNote: Leafy green; very friendly.

name: Waterleaf
logicNote: Leafy green; friendly.

name: Spinach (efo tete)
logicNote: Friendly leafy green.

name: Garden Egg
logicNote: Great low-sugar snack instead of sweet fruits.

name: Okra (vegetable)
logicNote: Often recommended for diabetics.

name: Cucumber
logicNote: Hydrating, almost no sugar.

name: Cabbage
logicNote: Low carb, high fibre.

name: Carrot
logicNote: Some natural sugar but low load; fine raw.

name: Tomato
logicNote: Friendly base for stews.

name: Green Beans
logicNote: Low carb, high fibre.

name: Ugba (oil bean)
logicNote: Fermented oil bean; low sugar.

name: Avocado (Pear)
logicNote: Healthy fat, almost no sugar. Great diabetic snack.

name: Pawpaw (Papaya)
logicNote: Friendlier fruit, but still natural sugar; keep portion small.

name: Watermelon
pairingAdvice: A few nuts to slow it.
logicNote: Mostly water but high GI; small portions only.

name: Orange
logicNote: Whole orange OK; orange juice is red.

name: Apple
logicNote: Lower GI fruit; the skin adds fibre.

name: Banana (ripe)
logicNote: The riper, the sweeter; keep it small.

name: Mango
logicNote: Sweet; small portions.

name: Pineapple
logicNote: High GI; keep portions tiny.

name: Guava
logicNote: High fibre, lower GI; a friendly fruit.

name: African Star Apple (Agbalumo / Udara)
logicNote: Tart but sugary; small portions.

name: Soursop
logicNote: Lower sugar fruit.

name: Dates (dabino)
logicNote: Natural but very concentrated sugar.

name: Cashew Fruit
logicNote: Juicy and sweet; small portions.

name: Groundnut (peanut)
pairingAdvice: Great alone or with garden egg.
logicNote: Protein and fat, slow rise. A smart snack swap.

name: Cashew Nut
logicNote: Healthy fat; keep portion small for calories.

name: Tiger Nut (whole)
logicNote: High fibre; the whole nut is friendlier than the sweet drink.

name: Coconut
logicNote: Low sugar; pairs well to slow corn.

name: Walnut (Nigerian, asala / ukpa)
logicNote: Friendly low-sugar snack.

name: Soft Drink (Coke / Fanta / Sprite)
logicNote: Liquid sugar; the fastest spike of all. The number one thing to drop.

name: Bottled Fruit Juice
logicNote: Even 100 percent juices are concentrated sugar without the fibre. Eat the whole fruit instead.

name: Malt Drink (Maltina / Amstel Malta)
logicNote: Often thought healthy but very high sugar.

name: Energy Drinks
logicNote: Sugar and caffeine; bad combination for blood sugar.

name: Zobo (no added sugar)
logicNote: Hibiscus drink; friendly only if no sugar is added.

name: Zobo (sweetened)
logicNote: The added sugar makes it as bad as a soft drink.

name: Kunu (sweetened)
logicNote: Grain drink; the sugar version spikes. Unsweetened millet kunu is gentler.

name: Tiger Nut Drink (Kunu Aya)
logicNote: Eat the whole tiger nut instead for more fibre.

name: Palm Wine
logicNote: Alcohol plus natural sugar; both affect blood sugar.

name: Soaked Garri (drink)
portionGuidance: Avoid, especially with sugar and milk.
pairingAdvice: If at all, with groundnut and no sugar.
logicNote: Cold soaked garri with sugar is a common hidden spike.

name: Milo / Bournvita / Ovaltine
logicNote: Chocolate malt drinks are mostly sugar.

name: Tea / Coffee (no sugar)
logicNote: Friendly without sugar or sweetened milk.

name: Water
logicNote: The best drink for blood sugar, full stop.

name: Milk (full cream, e.g. Peak)
pairingAdvice: In tea or oats, no sugar.
logicNote: Has natural milk sugar; avoid sweetened condensed milk, which is red.

name: Condensed Milk (sweetened)
logicNote: Loaded with added sugar.

name: Plain Yogurt (unsweetened)
logicNote: Unsweetened only; flavoured yogurts are full of sugar.

name: Sweetened / Flavoured Yogurt
logicNote: Often as sugary as a soft drink.

name: Wara (local cheese)
logicNote: Soft cheese; low carb, protein-rich.

name: Palm Oil
logicNote: No effect on blood sugar, but heavy for the heart; go easy.

name: Groundnut Oil / Vegetable Oil
logicNote: No sugar impact; watch total quantity.

name: Olive Oil
logicNote: Heart-friendly fat; no sugar impact.

name: Butter / Margarine
logicNote: No sugar impact; the bread under it is the issue.

name: Mayonnaise / Salad Cream
logicNote: Some sugar in salad cream; use lightly.

name: Sugar (table sugar)
logicNote: The single thing to cut first.

name: Honey
logicNote: Natural, but still pure sugar to your blood.

name: Glucose / Lucozade
portionGuidance: Avoid, unless treating a low-sugar emergency.
logicNote: Pure fast sugar. Only useful if blood sugar drops too low.

name: Salt
logicNote: No sugar impact, but too much raises blood pressure, which diabetics must watch.
